message:
style(fcp-core): apply rustfmt across pinned-test suite (no semantic changes)

Why
---
Sixteen test files in `crates/fcp-core/tests/` had drifted from the
project's rustfmt configuration. Most were authored under bead-driven
test-pin tasks that focused on what to assert rather than how the
file should be formatted, leaving lines that exceed the configured
line width, struct literals broken across one line where rustfmt
prefers multi-line, and conversely cases where rustfmt collapses a
multi-line literal back onto one line. Running rustfmt across them
brings the test suite back into a consistent canonical form so that
future agent diffs are signal (semantic test changes) rather than
noise (formatting churn that shows up alongside real edits).

These changes are entirely cosmetic: zero assertions added or
removed, zero fixture values changed, zero new tests, zero new
imports beyond what rustfmt re-orders.

What
----
Files touched - all pure rustfmt output, mechanically verifiable:

- crates/fcp-core/tests/build_environment_display.rs
- crates/fcp-core/tests/capability_proof_format.rs
- crates/fcp-core/tests/capability_type_serde_tag_matrix.rs
- crates/fcp-core/tests/compaction_level_ordering.rs
- crates/fcp-core/tests/connector_plan_step_ordering.rs
- crates/fcp-core/tests/connector_state_transitions.rs
- crates/fcp-core/tests/lease_holder_display_roundtrip.rs
- crates/fcp-core/tests/lease_token_format_invariants.rs
- crates/fcp-core/tests/manifest_bundle_hash_determinism.rs
- crates/fcp-core/tests/mesh_node_role_serde_tag.rs
- crates/fcp-core/tests/policy_router_route_key_dispatch.rs
- crates/fcp-core/tests/registry_acceptance_variants.rs
- crates/fcp-core/tests/retained_snapshot_serde_tag.rs
- crates/fcp-core/tests/schema_version_compatibility_relation.rs
- crates/fcp-core/tests/signed_package_catalog_serde_roundtrip.rs
- crates/fcp-core/tests/zone_route_display_serde.rs

Typical patterns:
- Multi-line `format!("blake3-256:{...}", "..".repeat(32))` collapsed
  to a single rustfmt-canonical line.
- `compute_policy_bundle_hash(a, b, c, d, e, f)` calls broken from
  inline form to one-arg-per-line where rustfmt prefers the
  vertical layout under the line-width limit.
- `PolicyEngine { zone_policy: policy }` struct literals expanded
  to multi-line form when the line exceeds the width budget.
- `SignedManifest::sign(schema, payload, key)` reformatted into
  rustfmt's canonical wrap when the let-binding pushes past
  the width limit.

Verification: re-running `cargo fmt --check` against this commit
produces no output. There is no path through these files where
behaviour can change as a result of rustfmt reflowing whitespace.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
